nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Beaumont
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Gilles, Gaetan, Honore, Jacques, Jean-Guy, Marcel.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of the five places in Normandy or several others elsewhere in France so named. The placename comes from Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + mont ‘hill’. There are also places in England so named under Norman influence in Cumberland, Lancashire, and Essex, the last of which changed its name in the 12th century from Fulepet ‘foul pit’ to Bealmont ‘beautiful hill’; these may also have given rise to the surname. The surname is now widespread throughout England, but most common in Yorkshire. Compare Belmont .

History: Many English bearers of this surname are descended from Henry de Beaumont (died 1340), who came to England from France as a soldier, serving Kings Edward I, II, and III of England. He acquired the earldom of Buchan in Scotland by marriage. — Many American bearers of this surname are descendants of John Beaumont (1612–47), who came to North America from England in 1630. William Beaumont came from England to Cambridge MA in 1635 and subsequently moved to CT.

variants.name: Bement
meanings: English (Cambridgeshire): variant of   Beaumont  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Beeman
meanings: English (Kent): occupational name for a beekeeper, from Middle English bee-man ‘bee-keeper’ (Old English bēo + mann).
Americanized form of German Biemann or of its Dutch cognate Bieman.

variants.name: Belmont
meanings: French and English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beaumont  .
Catalan: habitational name from the placename Bellmont ‘beautiful mountain’. Compare Spanish   Belmonte  .

variants.name: Baumunk
meanings: German: probably a Germanized form of a French Huguenot name Beaumont (see also Beaumont ).

variants.name: Bayman
meanings: Americanized form of German Beumann or Bäumann, variants of Baumann .

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

en.lifeSummary: Robert de Beaumont 3rd Earl Of Leicester was born in 1121, in Leicestershire, England as the son of Robert de Beaumont 2nd Lord of Leicester and Amice De Gael Heiress Of Breteuil. He married Petronella de Grandmesnil in 1155, in Leicester Abbey, Leicestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 4 daughters. He died on 31 August 1190, in Durrës, Durrës, Albania, at the age of 69.

Il a épousé Petronella de Grandmesnil en 1155, à Leicester Abbey, Leicestershire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 4 filles. ko.lifeSummary: Robert de Beaumont 3rd Earl Of Leicester(은)는 1121년 영국 레이스스터실르에서 Robert de Beaumont 2nd Lord of Leicester(와)과 Amice De Gael Heiress Of Breteuil의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1155년 영국 레이스스터실르 레이스스터 애브브이에서 Petronella de Grandmesnil(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1190년 8월 31일 알바니아 뒤e스 뒤e스에서 69세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Robert de Beaumont 3rd Earl Of Leicester родился в 1121 г. в местности лисестиршир, Англия. Его родителей звали Robert de Beaumont 2nd Lord of Leicester и Amice De Gael Heiress Of Breteuil. Он женился на женщине по имени Petronella de Grandmesnil в 1155 г. в местности лисестир эбби, лисестиршир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 4 дочери. Он умер 31 августа 1190 г. в местности дуррeс, дуррeс, Албания в возрасте 69 лет.
